violations.text: Portions of deli meat and cheeses were observed not marked with the date of opening. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Food items were marked with the date of opening during the time of inspection. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/02/2026

Utensils (bar tools) were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. Utensils between uses shall be stored in one of the following manners: on a dry clean surface, in water that is maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or more, or in water with sufficient flow to flush particulates to the drain. Utensils were cleaned and sanitized during the time of inspection and were placed on a dry clean surface. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/02/2026

The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned at a frequency necessary to prevent the accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 02/02/2026

Raw animal foods (ground beef) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (deli meat loaves) in the 2-door reach-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. The ground beef was moved to proper location during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/27/2025

Portions of deli meat and cheeses were observed not marked with the date of opening. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Food items were marked with the date of opening during the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/27/2025

The door to the outside, located in the back storage area, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 02/10/2025

Knives were observed stored in-between the prep unit and the prep sink. Utensils in-between uses shall be stored on a dry clean surface. Knives were cleaned and sanitized during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/27/2025

The exterior side door was observed to be not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Side door requires a door sweep. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2023

The floor and wall junctures behind sinks and dish machine at bar and righthand wall in kitchen including under 3 bay sink do not have coving. The floor and wall junctures must be coved and closed to no larger than 1/32nd of an inch. Coving shall be installed in these areas. New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2023

The flooring around mop sink is constructed of wood which is not durable, easily cleanable, or non-absorbent. The materials for indoor floors must be durable, easily cleanable and nonabsorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms. A smooth, durable, easily cleanable non-absorbent flooring shall be installed around mop sink. New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2023
